title: Credit enumerations
description: Enumerations specific to private credit facilities and instruments

FacilityStatus.description: Current lifecycle status of a credit facility. ACTIVE — facility is open and available for drawdowns. DRAWN — commitment has been fully or partially funded. FULLY_REPAID — all outstanding principal and interest have been repaid. DEFAULTED — borrower has failed to meet payment or covenant obligations. RESTRUCTURED — facility terms have been modified due to financial distress.

PaymentStatus.description: Status of a scheduled payment in an amortization schedule. SCHEDULED — payment is expected but not yet due or paid. PAID — payment has been received in full. OVERDUE — payment date has passed without receipt of funds. WAIVED — payment obligation has been forgiven or deferred by the lender.

ScheduleType.description: Repayment structure type for an amortization schedule. BULLET — entire principal repaid at maturity with no interim principal payments. AMORTIZING — principal repaid in regular installments over the life of the facility. CUSTOM — bespoke repayment schedule that does not follow a standard pattern. INTEREST_ONLY — only interest is paid during the term; principal is repaid at maturity.

CovenantType.description: Category of financial or operational covenant. LEVERAGE_RATIO — limits total debt relative to equity or EBITDA. INTEREST_COVERAGE — requires minimum ratio of earnings to interest expense. FIXED_CHARGE_COVERAGE — requires minimum ratio of cash flow to fixed charges. DEBT_TO_EBITDA — caps total debt as a multiple of EBITDA. MINIMUM_LIQUIDITY — requires the borrower to maintain a minimum cash or liquid asset balance. MINIMUM_REVENUE — sets a floor on periodic revenue. CAPEX_LIMIT — restricts capital expenditure to a maximum amount. INCURRENCE — tested only when a specific action is taken (e.g., incurring new debt). SPRINGING — dormant until a trigger condition is met, then actively tested. OTHER — any covenant type not captured by the standard categories.

ThresholdOperator.description: Comparison operator applied when testing an actual value against a covenant threshold. LTE — actual value must be less than or equal to the threshold. GTE — actual value must be greater than or equal to the threshold. LT — actual value must be strictly less than the threshold. GT — actual value must be strictly greater than the threshold. EQ — actual value must equal the threshold exactly.

TestingFrequency.description: How often a covenant is tested for compliance. MONTHLY — tested once per calendar month. QUARTERLY — tested once per fiscal quarter. SEMI_ANNUAL — tested twice per fiscal year. ANNUAL — tested once per fiscal year.

RateIndex.description: Reference rate index for floating-rate instruments
